analytics.addLeadCaptureHint: Add a Lead Capture slide to your presentation to start collecting contact information from viewers.
analytics.shareToGetViewers: Once people view your presentation, their sessions will appear here.
analytics.shareToGetViews: Share your presentation to start seeing view analytics here.
analytics.viewsNeededForEngagement: Once viewers start watching your presentation, you'll see which slides get the most attention.
comments.deleteOriginalPrompt: New slide added. Do you want to delete the original slide {num}?
comments.empty.noSlide: Select a slide to see its comments
comments.empty.none: No comments yet. Be the first to add one!
comments.filter.title: Filter comments
comments.link.invalid: Use an http://, https:// or mailto: address
comments.link.message: Paste a URL. It will show as a link.
comments.pinModeHint: Click on the slide to place comment
comments.scope.label: Comments scope
comments.title: Comments
dataSource.bindings.hint: Map source data to slide fields. Use row[0].PropertyName for databases, or A1/B2 for CSV cells.
dataSource.connect.title: Bind this slide to live data from Notion, CSV, or an API
dataSource.disconnect.title: Remove data source connection
dataSource.modal.hint: Bind live data to this {type} slide
dataSource.modal.title: Connect Data Source
editor.aiAppend.confirmDiscard: You have entered text. Close this wizard and discard your input?
editor.aiAppend.help: Describe what you want to add. You can request one or multiple slides at once. The AI also sees the existing presentation and tries to fit in logically. If you ask for a photo/image, an existing background image will be used as a placeholder (which you can replace afterwards).
editor.aiAppend.langHint.en: Language mode: English (UK) (AI output will be English).
editor.aiAppend.langHint.nl: Language mode: Dutch (AI output will be Dutch).
editor.aiAppend.placeholder:
E.g.
- Add 2 slides about approach and planning
- Add a slide with a photo (placeholder) and 4 bullet points with key messages
- Add a “list” slide with 6 tips (bullets)
- Add a chart slide with a bar chart based on these numbers: ...
editor.aiAppend.required: First describe what you want to add.
editor.aiAppend.title: AI: add slides
editor.aiAppend.verbatim: Use my text verbatim (only fix typos, don’t rewrite)
editor.aiAppend.verbatimHint: Keeps your wording as-is. Your text may mix instructions with the copy to place on the slides; the AI follows the instructions but quotes the copy instead of rephrasing it.
editor.aiReview.closeConfirm: Close the review and discard the generated slides? Nothing has been added to the deck yet.
editor.aiReview.feedbackLabel: Want something different? Describe it and adjust the batch
editor.aiReview.feedbackPlaceholder: E.g. make it 3 slides, turn the list into a timeline, shorter texts…
editor.aiReview.hint: Nothing has been added yet. Inspect the proposed slides, switch types where useful, then accept or adjust the batch.
editor.aiReview.title: Review AI slides
editor.alt.help: Describe what's important in the image (not the slide title). Aim for ~120–180 characters.
editor.analyzeModal.complete: Analysis complete! {count} suggestions added as comments.
editor.analyzeModal.hint: AI will analyze your presentation and create improvement suggestions as comments.
editor.analyzeModal.info: AI will review your slides and suggest improvements for clarity, structure, and visual balance. Suggestions appear as comments you can accept, dismiss, or act on.
editor.analyzeModal.noSuggestions: No suggestions found. Your presentation looks great!
editor.analyzeModal.title: AI Analysis
editor.authorLocked.banner: This slide is locked by the author
editor.autoFit.applied: Switched to "Fit (no crop)" to show your full image. You can change this in Layout.
editor.bulkEdit.hint: The slide updates live while you type. Layout, background and accessibility stay in the side panel.
editor.bulkEdit.openTitle: Edit all text fields of this slide in one view
editor.bulkEdit.title: Edit all text
editor.cards.label: Label
editor.cards.linkHelp: Makes the card clickable. Use #3 to jump to slide 3, or a full https:// / mailto: link (opens in a new tab).
editor.cards.linkHelp2: Makes the card clickable. Pick a slide to jump to, or type an https:// / mailto: link (opens in a new tab).
editor.cards.title: Cards
editor.changeTheme.optionConvertDesc: Automatically convert slides to compatible types and change theme
editor.changeTheme.optionExportDesc: Download presentation as JSON before making changes
editor.changeTheme.optionProceedDesc: Change theme without converting slides (they may look different)
editor.changeTheme.optionReviewDesc: Close this modal and navigate to the first affected slide
editor.changeTheme.warningHint: These slides use features that work differently in the new theme.
editor.chart.grid.helpBarPie: A label and a numeric value per row. Paste a block from Sheets/Excel into any cell.
editor.chart.grid.helpLine: X label in the first column, then one or two numeric series. The header row sets the series names.
editor.chart.help.barPie:
Paste TSV (from Sheets/Excel) or CSV.
Bar/Pie format: Label, Value.
Example:
Label,Value
A,10
B,25
C,15
editor.chart.help.line:
Paste TSV (from Sheets/Excel) or CSV.
Line format: X, Series 1, optional Series 2.
Use the header row to set series names, or fill “Series 1 label (legend)” / “Series 2 label (legend)”.
Example:
X,Revenue,Cost
Jan,12,8
Feb,18,11
editor.code.readOnly: Read-only. You do not have permission to edit raw HTML/CSS.
editor.collab.liveEditsUnavailable: Live collaboration failed to load; changes are not being saved. Reload the editor.
editor.companion.title: Open speaker notes companion on your phone (QR code).
editor.conflict.help: Reload to get the latest version. If you’re worried about losing your changes: copy your version as JSON first.
editor.conflict.hintFallback: This presentation was saved by someone else.
editor.conflict.title: Could not save (conflict)

editor.deckSettings.autoAdvance.modePaci: Pacing timer (shows timer, you advance manually)

editor.deckSettings.lang.help: Used for public sharing and exports (HTML lang attribute).
editor.deckSettings.lang.title: Document language
editor.deckSettings.liveVideo.title: Live Video

editor.deckSettings.liveVideo.urlHelp: Paste a YouTube Live, Vimeo, Bunny, Mux, Cloudflare, or HLS stream URL.
editor.deckSettings.qa.help: When disabled, Q&A is hidden in follow-along (participants can’t ask questions).
editor.deckSettings.qa.title: Enable Q&A
editor.deckSettings.rssFeed.help: When checked, this presentation will not appear in the public RSS feed.
editor.deckSettings.rssFeed.title: Exclude from RSS feed

editor.deckSettings.steps.title: Steps (reveal content step-by-step)

editor.deckSettings.tags.unavailable: Tags are not available in this mode.

editor.deckSettings.transitions.help: Presenter only. Editor and follow-along remain static by default.
editor.deckSettings.transitions.title: Presenter transition (slide → slide)
editor.descriptionModal.hint: This becomes the public meta description and is included in integrations. Keep it short: exactly two sentences.
editor.export.pdfFailed: PDF render failed or timed out. Print it in the browser instead:
editor.export.publishHint: Want a link that stays current and revocable? Publish it from the Share menu.
editor.export.title: Export to file
editor.followInvite.explanation: This "Follow-along invite" slide shows a QR code that lets your audience follow along in their own language and respond to interactive slides (polls, feedback). The slide is left out of publishing and exports. You can switch it off; it then stays in the list (greyed out) but is skipped while presenting.
editor.followInviteSuggest.description: You\'re adding an interactive slide that requires audience participation. To let your audience join, you need a "Follow along" slide with a QR code. Would you like to add one?
editor.followInviteSuggest.title: Add a QR code slide?
editor.freeform.emptyState: No elements yet. Click the buttons above to add headings, text, or images.

editor.image.focusGridHelp: Drag the point on the image, or pick a position here.
editor.image.help.storage: Images are stored locally in /server/uploads and used via URL.
editor.image.help.uploadsDisabled: Choose from the library (recommended). Uploads are disabled.

editor.imagePosition.cropHelp: Pick what should stay visible when the image is cropped.

editor.imagePosition.widerThanBox: Image is wider than the box: choose top/center/bottom.

editor.images.upload.help: Upload custom logos (they will be added to the selection)
editor.images.uploadsDisabled: Uploads are disabled; use the library.

editor.importSlides.dropzone.text: Drop PDF here or click to upload
editor.importSlides.help: Import slides from a PDF file. Each page will be converted to an image slide at 1920x1080 resolution.

editor.inline.media.dropInvalid: That is not an image file.

editor.publish.confirm:
Publish?

This makes the presentation publicly accessible to anyone with the link.
editor.publish.missingAltConfirm:
Warning: these slides contain an image without alt text (NL/EN): {slides}

Publish anyway?
editor.publish.notion.confirm:
Add presentation embed to the source Notion page?

This will add a divider, heading, and embedded presentation at the bottom of the Notion page.
editor.publish.notion.notAvailable: Notion publishing not available for this presentation.
editor.publish.rssFeedNotice: This presentation will appear in your public RSS feed. You can exclude it in Deck Settings.
editor.publish.unpublish.confirm:
Unpublish?

This will invalidate the public link and embed links. Anyone with a shared /p/ or /embed/ link will no longer be able to open the presentation.

If you use this link in a website, invite, follow-along, notes/QR or other tooling, it will stop working there too.
editor.publishModal.help: Copy the public link or embed code below. Tip: the public link is already on your clipboard.
editor.publishModal.previewHint: Preview image is generated from your first slide.
editor.publishModal.previewHintDefault: A default preview image is used.
editor.publishModal.slugHint: Tip: existing links will keep working (they redirect to the new slug).
editor.publishModal.title: Published
